name: Tamurejo, Garbayuela y Valdemanco del Esteras — circular from Siruela
name_original: Tamurejo, Garbayuela y Valdemanco del Esteras — circular desde Siruela
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Siruela traverse a varied landscape characterized by hilly terrain, large reservoirs, and the unique *dehesa* ecosystem. Situated in the Extremadura region, the area features undulating routes with significant elevation changes, offering both moderate climbs and more demanding sections. Cyclists can expect scenic views of the Zújar and La Serena reservoirs, alongside oak forests and open plains. The topography provides diverse cycling experiences suitable for various fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Siruela The most popular road cycling route is Tamurejo, Garbayuela y Valdemanco del Esteras — circular from Siruela, a 36.8 miles (59.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castle of Puebla de Alcocer – Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer loop from Siruela, a moderate 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route features views of the historic Castle of Puebla de Alcocer and surrounding landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the View of Cerro Masatrigo – Capilla and its Castle loop from Puebla de Alcocer, a 50.8 miles (81.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Road cycling around Siruela is defined by hilly topography, scenic reservoir views, and the distinctive *dehesa* ecosystems. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Siruela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Siruela?
A: Road cycling routes around Siruela feature a varied and engaging landscape. You can expect undulating terrain with significant elevation changes, including moderate climbs and some more demanding sections. The region is characterized by its proximity to the Zújar and La Serena reservoirs, offering picturesque views of the water, and the unique &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; ecosystem with its oak forests and open plains.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Siruela?
A: There are 10 road cycling routes available around Siruela documented on komoot. These routes offer diverse experiences, catering to various fitness levels from moderate to difficult.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Siruela?
A: Yes, Siruela offers several challenging road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cerro Masatrigo – Capilla and its Castle loop from Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 50.8-mile (81.7 km) trail with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128549" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Ríofrío – Puerto del Rayo loop from Siruela&lt;/a&gt;, covering 86.7 km with similar elevation.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Siruela?
A: While the routes around Siruela tend to feature significant elevation changes, there are moderate options that can be suitable for beginners with a reasonable level of fitness. Currently, there are no routes classified as 'easy' in the guide, but the moderate routes offer a good starting point to experience the region's varied terrain.

Q: What scenic viewpoints or natural features can I expect to see on road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes in Siruela offer stunning natural scenery. You'll frequently encounter panoramic views of the large Zújar and La Serena reservoirs. The routes also traverse the distinctive &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; ecosystems, characterized by oak forests, providing a unique and pleasant backdrop for your ride. A notable natural feature is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839713" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, which offers impressive vistas.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region around Siruela is rich in history. Cyclists can pass by historical landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5460742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer – Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer loop from Siruela&lt;/a&gt; route. The broader Extremadura region also boasts a rich history with Roman influences and historic towns.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available around Siruela?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Siruela are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992994320/tamurejo-garbayuela-y-valdemanco-del-esteras-circular-from-siruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tamurejo, Garbayuela y Valdemanco del Esteras — circular from Siruela&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Puebla de Alcocer – Castillo de Puebla de Alcocer loop from Siruela&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take around Siruela?
A: The duration of road cycling routes around Siruela varies depending on the distance and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992994320/tamurejo-garbayuela-y-valdemanco-del-esteras-circular-from-siruela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tamurejo, Garbayuela y Valdemanco del Esteras — circular from Siruela&lt;/a&gt; route, at 59.2 km, typically takes around 2 hours 48 minutes. Longer, more challenging routes can take up to 4 hours or more, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24527487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cerro Masatrigo – Capilla and its Castle loop from Puebla de Alcocer&lt;/a&gt; which is often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes.

Q: What is the best season for road cycling in Siruela?
A: Extremadura, where Siruela is located, generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often considered ideal, with milder temperatures and pleasant weather. Summers can be very hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during this season. Winters are generally mild, though some days can be cooler.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for families in Siruela?
A: While the routes listed in this guide are primarily moderate to difficult with significant elevation, the broader region of Extremadura is known for its extensive network of roads and trails. Families looking for easier rides might find suitable, less challenging sections on local, quieter roads, though specific 'family-friendly' designated road cycling routes are not detailed in the provided data.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Siruela?
A: Road cyclists using komoot to explore Siruela's varied terrain often praise the region's hilly topography, the scenic views of the reservoirs, and the distinctive &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; ecosystems. The diverse network of routes offers options for different ability levels, making it an appealing destination for many.
tips: Valdemanco del Esteras is a Spanish municipality in the province of Ciudad Real, in the autonomous community of Castilla-La Mancha, however, the town is located on a beautiful road where the province of Badajoz begins.
Beautiful town that is located at the foot of a small hill called Pinguta del Morro and that is also located south of the Río Siruela.
Population that is below the Sierra de los Villares and crossed by the Río Guadalemar. One of the most iconic elements of the center is its church, the Ermita de San Blas.
